title: Charge of the 24th and 25th Colored Infantry and Rescue of the Rough Riders at San Juan Hill, July 2nd, 1898
description: The Battle of San Juan Hill brought fame to Col. Theodore Roosevelt and his Rough Riders. But if not for the charge of the 10th Cavalry and 24th Infantry regiments, Roosevelt's troops might have been defeated. Although the 25th Infantry did not charge up San Juan Hill with the 24th as depicted here - it was fighting the Battle of El Caney at the time - this print nevertheless captures African American participation on San Juan Hill.

title: Cabinet card of Carrie
description: A faded cabinet card photograph of a little girl named Carrie taken by Johnson Photography in Chicago, IL. She is captured sitting on the edge of wicker-like furniture and wearing a light color dress with two bows on either side of her hair. At the bottom margin is text identifying the photographer [Johnson / 113 East Adams / Chicago]. The back has handwritten text reading [From Carrie L-[undetermined] / to / Cousin Leroy Bone].

title: Nation of Islam Sister Clara Muhammad School uniform beret worn by Medina Rowell
description: A dark brown beret, part of a Sister Clara Muhammad School uniform, worn by Medina Rowell. The polyester beret is round with an oblong opening on the underside for the wearer's head.

title: Seven piece place setting of stainless steel flatware sold by Salaam
description: Seven (7) piece place setting of stainless steel flatware purchased from Salaam International in Chicago, Illinois, in 1972 by Khalid and Patricia Al Amin. Each piece has a repeating design of stylized leaves and dots on the front handle and is marked on the verso handle "SALAAM STAINLESS KOREA" except the table knife (.3) which is marked on the verso blade. The pieces include:

title: Napkin from the Shabazz Restaurant
description: This rectangular Shabazz Restaurant paper napkin is off white with a light brown horizontal rectangular field on the lower third portion of the front. Within the field are white letters that read "SHABAZZ/ RESTAURANT/ 616 EAST 71TH ST. PHONE 483-1688." There is a 7-pointed star on the left side of the logo, inside the first 'S.'

title: Robe worn by Floyd Patterson for World Heavyweight Title against Sonny Liston
description: A blue and orange satin boxing robe worn by Floyd Patterson. The body of the robe is blue with orange accents around at the cuffs, pocket, and lapel that continues down the front opening. The robe fastens with an orange satin sash and has three pockets on the front, one on either side of the waist and one on the proper left breast. The breast pocket has [NY] sewn to it in stylized orange letters. The upper back of the robe reads: [FLOYD / PATTERSON], also in stitched orange letters. A manufacturer's tag is sewn to the interior at the nape of the neck.

title: Postcard of Duke Ellington and his Orchestra, with autograph
description: A black and white photographic postcard of Duke Ellington and his orchestra.

title: Folding tab button for Kennedy - Johnson 1960 presidential campaign
description: A 1960 presidential campaign folding tab, metal button for John F. Kennedy and running mate Lyndon B. Johnson. Kennedy ran as a candidate of the Democratic Party. The button has red, white, and blue horizontal bands. The central white band has blue text that reads, [KENNEDY / and / JOHNSON]. Two (2), white five pointed stars are printed in the red and blue bands above and below the candidates’ names. The folding portion of the button is white and has the manufacturer’s information, union information and logos printed in blue. There are three union labels and the name of the manufacturer printed on the folding tab of the button. The back of the button is silver toned.

title: Nation of Islam Sister Clara Muhammad School uniform beret worn by Medina Rowell
description: A dark brown beret, part of a Sister Clara Muhammad School uniform, worn by Medina Rowell. The wool beret is round with an oblong opening on the underside for the wearer's head.

title: Juba (Dance)
description: Eight page sheet music booklet for “Juba” (Dance) from the suite "In The Bottoms," composed by R. Nathaniel Dett. Printed in black ink on off-white paper, the front cover features a circular inset illustration of a banjo player, dancer, and another individual. The text reads [As / played by the / world renowned pianist / Percy Grainger / “Juba” / (dance) / From the Suite / “In the Bottoms” for the Piano / by / R. Nathaniel Dett / Price 60¢ / NET SCHEDULE / CHICAGO / CLAYTON F. SUMMY CO. / WEEKES & CO LONDON]. The interior pages features one full page of text on "In The Bottoms," written by R. Nathaniel Dett followed by five interior pages of printed sheet music. The back cover is a horizontal sheet music for songs published by Clayton F. Summy Co.

title: Service card of Pullman porter Percy Davis
description: An off-white paper service card with black printed text used by Percy Davis. The card is laminated. The card reads “THIS CAR IS SERVED BY / P DAVIS / PORTER / THE PULLMAN COMPANY / CHICAGO.” There is no printing on the reverse.

title: Order form from Newsflash, Inc
